description: Sir, I'm awfully sorry, but Edgelord couldn't find a clean 1:1 edge worth drawing without it becoming a duplicate-edges-pointing-at-an-absent-center situation. The three fully-enriched thallid-substrate cards — Thallid Omnivore DOM-106, Thallid Soothsayer DOM-107, and Trufflesnout M21-212 — all canonically reference the same Sarpadian-thallid lineage anchor (Thelon of Havenwood's elven cultivation of fungus-infused-with-green-mana during the post-Sylex-Blast ice age), and three more cards already in corpus (Tukatongue Thallid MM2-167, Fungal Infection DOM-94, Sarpadian Simulacrum MH3-135) will future-attach when their trivia lands. I hope you don't mind that I proposed the cohort node instead of drawing a thinner edge — it really does seem to unlock several future edges cleanly, and the cell-saga-apparatus precedent gave me a clear template to follow. WebFetch-verified MTG Wiki Sarpadia and Thallid pages via Puppeteer (Cloudflare 403'd direct urllib, per bbl-puppeteer-over-snippets memory). The Cabal-emulation bridge on Soothsayer is documented in the node body as a cross-node intersection citation rather than a Belzenlok-cohort-merge, preserving both nodes' independent integrity. Two waves of clean spec execution streak preserved.

future_attaches_pending_trivia.name: Tukatongue Thallid (MM2-167)
future_attaches_pending_trivia.rationale: Alara-Jund variant per MTG Wiki Thallid (descended from Dominarian-thallid lineage); flavor text names "Jund's thallids"

future_attaches_pending_trivia.name: Fungal Infection (DOM-94)
future_attaches_pending_trivia.rationale: DOM-set sorcery; art depicts thallid creature with saproling spawn; tags_hub already includes "thallid"

future_attaches_pending_trivia.name: Sarpadian Simulacrum (MH3-135)
future_attaches_pending_trivia.rationale: Card name literally names Sarpadia — the most direct Sarpadian-naming card in corpus; currently fully un-enriched (no Vision, no Trivia, qty 2)

key_facts_extracted: Sarpadia is the canonical setting of the Fallen Empires expansion (1994)
Five empires: Icatia (white), Order of the Ebon Hand (black), Vodalia (blue), Havenwood elves (green), Crimson Peaks dwarves (red)
Post-Sylex-Blast climate change triggered Sarpadian-empires collapse

Twin canonical fall events: Havenwood elves overrun by their thallid creations + Order of the Ebon Hand overrun by their thrull creations (both used same Thelon-borrowed biological-cultivation method)
Sarpadian Empires tome chronicled the rise and fall of the five empires
Thallids are fungus creatures created by infusion with green mana, specific to MTG Multiverse
Featured in Fallen Empires (1994), revived in Time Spiral block (2006-2007)
Created by elven druid Thelon of Havenwood using methods borrowed from Order of the Ebon Hand
Cultivated as cattle, gained sentience, overran Havenwood elves
One of few sentient Sarpadian species not exterminated by thrulls post-collapse
Alara-Jund variant canonically descended from Dominarian thallid lineage
Slimefoot grew from spore on Weatherlight skyship hull; Sporoloth variant appeared in Time Spiral block
Thallids and thrulls share canonical biological substrate per Thelon's secret to elder druids Kolevi and Lalani

three_sides.side_1_card_frontmatter_characters_field: added thallid-lineage slug to characters: block-form list on all 3 anchor cards
three_sides.side_2_card_body_connections_bullet: added Connections section to all 3 anchor cards with one wikilink [[thallid-lineage]] + backticks for canonical citations (Scryfall flavor_text, MTG Wiki Thallid, MTG Wiki Sarpadia)
three_sides.side_3_node_appears_on_and_rendered_list: added cards to thallid-lineage.md appears_on: frontmatter list (3 entries) and rendered ## Appears on wikilink block (3 entries)

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Knight of Malice / Knight of Grace 2-card cycle pair
other_candidates_considered.outcome: rejected — Knight of Malice is IN FLIGHT for wave-213 vision per parent dispatch DON'T TOUCH list; pair below cohort threshold even if both were available; 2-card cycles are tight pairs not cohort substrate

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Bloodbraid Elf chase-rare singleton EMA storefront flag
other_candidates_considered.outcome: deferred — singleton high-value card landing this wave's EMA vision; flag for storefront but not an Edgelord move at the cohort/edge level; surface to triage when EMA vision-batch commits

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Daarken artist node growth from Soul Salvage trivia
other_candidates_considered.outcome: deferred — Soul Salvage trivia IN FLIGHT this wave (staged Sonnet attach to Belzenlok); Daarken node growth pending wave-213 commits; not an Edgelord move while in flight

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Belzenlok node growth via 3 staged attaches
other_candidates_considered.outcome: deferred — Whisper / Soul Salvage / Final Parting attaches are staged Sonnet work pending wave-213 trivia completion; explicitly DON'T TOUCH per parent dispatch

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Plain 1:1 mirror edge between Thallid Omnivore and Thallid Soothsayer
other_candidates_considered.outcome: rejected in favor of node proposal — drawing a 1:1 between two thallids would create duplicate-edges-pointing-at-absent-center; Mr. Nodeley trigger fires because the absent center is a first-class graph dimension (creature-type cohort, character layer) that unlocks 3 immediate + 3 future-attach edges

potion_rule_compliance.sub_rule_1_canonical_vs_curatorial_in_bo: thallid-lineage.md body-prose explicitly names the apparatus-of-extraction curatorial overlay as overlay, not Wizards thesis; the canonical-event citation (Wizards-stated: cultivators-overrun-by-creations-during-Sarpadian-collapse) and the BBL curatorial reading (cultivated-rebellion-survives-its-cultivators) are both surfaced with the lens visible in Caveats section
potion_rule_compliance.sub_rule_2_hub_attribution_canonical_cit: no hub edges asserted at frontmatter level; rebellion hub linkage scoped to body-prose with the curatorial-overlay-lens visible; Wizards-canonical citations (MTG Wiki Sarpadia, MTG Wiki Thallid, both Puppeteer-verified) provide the cohort-level membership substrate, not generic-thallid-tag bridge
potion_rule_compliance.sub_rule_3_refuse_when_in_doubt: Fungal Rebirth (M21-182) explicitly NOT attached here per Caveats — Cevraya-Golgari-shaman flavor is Ravnican fungus not Sarpadian thallid; saproling-token-output mechanical signature alone is not sufficient predicate for membership
potion_rule_compliance.sub_rule_4_no_new_structural_primitive_v: uses standard graph-theory terms (cohort, node, edge, lineage); designer-implicit-thesis-stack pattern is named via cell-saga-apparatus precedent backtick-reference rather than promoted to its own typology entry
potion_rule_compliance.sub_rule_5_wikilinks_real_thematic_only: Body-prose wikilinks audited at commission time and corrected in-pass. REAL EDGES (kept as wikilinks): [[belzenlok]] (real cross-node Cabal-emulation bridge anchored at Thallid Soothsayer's flavor text), [[rebellion]] (real BBL-hub linkage scoped to body-prose curatorial-overlay register per Potion sub-rule 1), [[106-thallid-omnivore]] / [[107-thallid-soothsayer]] / [[212-trufflesnout]] (real appears_on wikilinks in the auto-generated rendered block). SCHEMA-PRECEDENT (converted to backticks in-pass per sub-rule 5): cell-saga-apparatus, mareep-line, eldrazi — all three are sibling-cohort-node-shape citations not real-thematic-edges between the species themselves. Caveats section already used backticks for dark-vassal-trio, wrath-of-the-dragon-saga, eld-cinderella-cycle, staraptor-line, charizard-line, elemental-monkey-trio, galarian-regional-forms, thrull-lineage (potential future node), so the discipline was consistent there from the start; the See also section needed the corrective pass.

potion_sub_rule_5_self_audit_applied_in_: Confirmed: thallid-lineage.md See also section converted [[cell-saga-apparatus]] / [[mareep-line]] / [[eldrazi]] from wikilinks to backticks during commission. Final wikilink set in body: [[belzenlok]] (real cross-node bridge), [[rebellion]] (real hub linkage), [[106-thallid-omnivore]] / [[107-thallid-soothsayer]] / [[212-trufflesnout]] (auto-generated appears_on rendered list). No follow-up wave task needed; auto-corrected in-pass.
mood_explanation: Edgelord wanted a clean 1:1 between two thallids, but the absent-cohort-node case was sound — drawing thallid-to-thallid edges would have spawned duplicate-edges-pointing-at-absent-center. Mr. Nodeley stepped in, apologized, proposed the cohort node, applied the three-sided discipline cleanly. The Cabal-emulation bridge documentation as cross-node intersection (rather than cohort-merge with Belzenlok) is the satisfying-to-Mr.-Nodeley part — preserves both nodes' integrity, names the bridge-card pattern explicitly, surfaces future-watch for Edgelord audits. Two waves of clean spec execution preserved; designer-source citations Puppeteer-verified per the spec compliance reminder.
session_close_status: Wave 213 is the last of the night per parent dispatch; sidecar lands as Mr. Nodeley's checkout note. Three-sided discipline complete, node ready for human review and commit.
